headline: PPPA amendment restricts 14 more online publications
context: The Printing Presses and Publications Act was amended in March 2025 to extend licensing requirements to digital-only news outlets.

cards.big: 14 online news portals ordered to obtain PPPA licences by June 2025
cards.sub: Non-compliance carries fines up to RM20,000 and 3 years jail

cards.big: PPPA Section 3 grants Home Minister absolute discretion over licences
cards.sub: No judicial review available for licence denial under current law

cards.big: Press Freedom Index ranked Malaysia 107th out of 180 countries in 2025
cards.sub: Licensing regime cited as key factor in declining press freedom

cards.big: 73% of Malaysians now rely on online sources for news daily
cards.sub: Digital media regulation directly affects information access for millions

cards.big: Regulating misinformation or controlling the narrative on dissent?
Centre for Independent Journalism documented 8 licence rejections since 2023
